task.objective: Run a deep deterministic assurance campaign against the Phase 1 synthetic kernel, autonomously discover and remediate in-scope defects, and materially increase evidence for determinism, boundary safety, lineage integrity, Verdict reduction, and immutability without widening product scope.

forbidden: network access from kernel tests or production code

requirements: Treat this as an autonomous assurance and convergence campaign, not a scripted test-writing task. Continue until BUILDER_READY_FOR_REVIEW or a true stop condition is reached.

Build a deterministic, dependency-free assurance architecture inside packages/kernel/test that can generate broad synthetic in-memory evaluation cases without time, random, network, filesystem, environment, repository inspection, or model input.
Exercise at least 256 deterministically identified assurance cases across valid and malformed kernel inputs. Case identity and generation order must be stable and reviewable.

Cover permutation invariance for claims, evidenceContracts, contract requirementIds, evidenceRequirements, observations, observation limitations, and rules wherever the collection is semantically unordered.
Prove repeated evaluation and semantically equivalent input permutations produce deeply equal finalized bundles and stable bundle identities.

Prove primitive distinction and canonical serialization behavior across null, booleans, strings, finite numbers, negative zero where relevant, nested object key order, and normalized array order without inventing new product semantics.
Exercise missing Evidence Requirement classification and deterministic reason retention across multiple simultaneous missing requirements.
Exercise Rule denial matrices using EVIDENCE_PRESENT and EVIDENCE_ABSENT, multiple triggered and non-triggered Rules, mixed reason-code ordering, and lower-precedence reason retention.

Cross-check Verdict reduction against an independently implemented test-only reference precedence model for all non-empty combinations of the four canonical Verdict values up to a bounded campaign size chosen by the Builder.
Verify reduction preserves all applicable reason codes and candidate lineage references in deterministic order while selecting BLOCKED > REJECTED > REVISION_REQUIRED > ADMISSIBLE.
Build recursive boundary-shape assurance across the authoritative graph. Systematically place malformed structures at distinct modeled paths rather than testing only top-level records.

Boundary assurance must materially cover Proxy wrappers, revoked Proxy, accessors, symbols, non-enumerable properties, sparse Arrays, non-ordinary Array prototypes, Array subclasses, unknown fields, forbidden authority-shaped fields, __proto__ own keys, cycles, Date, Map, Set, function, bigint, undefined, NaN, Infinity, duplicate identities, invalid references, and invalid provenance at representative root and nested paths.

For executable-wrapper regressions, prove caller-controlled getter, Array override, custom prototype method, and Proxy trap execution counters remain zero before rejection where the current boundary promises early rejection.
Exercise caller-input isolation with multiple post-evaluation mutations to nested caller arrays and records and prove finalized bundle content and identity do not change.

Exercise deep immutability over every reachable object and Array in representative ADMISSIBLE, REVISION_REQUIRED, and REJECTED bundles. Attempts to mutate nested finalized structures must fail or leave the bundle unchanged according to strict-mode JavaScript behavior.
Verify Evidence identity, acceptedObservationIds, requirement identity, evaluation identity, and lineage references remain deterministic across repeated and permuted equivalent inputs.

Verify Evidence Lineage contains the required Claim, selected Evidence Contract, contract selection provenance, Evidence Requirement, accepted Observation, produced Evidence, Rule, candidate classification, and Verdict reduction references for representative evaluations.
Verify unrelated Observation scopes remain rejected while valid-scope unmatched Observations remain accepted boundary input and only affect Evidence satisfaction according to existing semantics.
Verify Claim text cannot substitute for Observation facts under adversarial matching text and value combinations.
Verify modelConfidence, inferenceProposal, and proposedContent cannot enter the authoritative path when attached at representative record and Array container paths.
Verify KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING remains the single kernel-owned missing-Evidence condition code and cannot be supplied by a Rule. Preserve HARN_ namespace rejection.

Audit the assurance campaign for vacuous tests, duplicated cases that add no new path or invariant, false-positive pass conditions, skipped or todo tests, assertions that only prove no throw, and test names or comments being treated as authority.

When the assurance campaign discovers a defect in packages/kernel that violates existing authoritative semantics or this task's invariants, autonomously diagnose, remediate within writable scope, add a focused regression, and rerun the full campaign. Do not ask for approval for reversible in-scope remediation.

Do not change packages/contracts or authoritative product semantics to make a failing assurance case pass. If a genuine protocol, terminology, Trust, Verdict, or contract-shape change is required, stop and report BLOCKED with the exact boundary conflict.

Keep production implementation small and coherent. Do not add a testing framework, fuzzing dependency, random generator package, snapshot framework, build output, generated runtime artifact, or general DSL.

Create docs/engineering/kernel-assurance-campaign.md as an implementation and assurance record, not product authority. Document campaign architecture, deterministic case identity, invariant families, reference-model boundary, defect remediation loop, non-goals, and known gaps.

Create a Builder assurance review under docs/reviews. Record every Builder-discovered finding with ID, severity CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW, source, affected path, observation, risk, remediation, validation, and disposition FIXED/OPEN/DEFERRED_WITH_REASON/BLOCKED.
Fix all Builder-discovered CRITICAL and HIGH findings that are remediable within scope. Do not delete, skip, weaken, or replace failing assurance cases with empty assertions.

Record complete KERNEL-ASSURE-001 validation evidence, including actual case count, invariant-family counts, test count, repeated campaign results, defect findings, remediation commits if any, verify no-mutation comparison, scope inspection, and forbidden production-source search.
Do not claim Phase 1 completion, product readiness, independent acceptance, PASS, or ADMISSIBLE as Builder task status.

requiredInvariants: Assurance case generation is deterministic and stable across repeated runs.
At least 256 deterministically identified assurance cases are exercised.
No test or production path uses time, randomness, network, repository inspection, filesystem state, environment values, or model output as authoritative evaluation input.
Semantically equivalent normalized inputs produce deeply equal bundles and stable bundle identities.
Verdict reduction matches the test-only reference precedence model and preserves lower-precedence reasons and lineage references.
Executable caller-controlled wrappers cannot execute before early boundary rejection where promised by the current kernel boundary.
Caller input remains unfrozen and unmodified, and post-evaluation caller mutation cannot alter finalized bundles.
Representative finalized bundles are deeply immutable at every reachable object and Array.
KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING remains kernel-owned and Rule-supplied use remains rejected.
HARN_ Rule reason codes remain rejected.
No production package outside contracts and kernel exists.
No repository inspection, target-code execution, verification execution, Policy runtime, adapter, delivery, network, or inference path is introduced.

requiredArtifacts: at least 256 stable identified assurance cases
caller isolation and deep immutability assurance
stopConditions: a new external dependency is required
a new production package is required
repository inspection product behavior is required
verification execution product behavior is required
adapter or Adapter Capability implementation is required
delivery or integration surface work is required
repeated autonomous remediation reaches a deadlock without converging
